content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

SUPERSEDEI
by A-96-103(willet)

April 14, 1994

Robert M. Hertzberg
Margolis, Hertzberg & Morin
Attorneys at Law
3550 Wilshire Boulevard, Suite 1418
Los Angeles, CA 90010

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-109

Dear Mr. Hertzberg:

You are requesting confirmation of oral advice which you
recently received from Ms. Dixie Howard in our Technical
Assistance and Analysis Division. You formed a committee for the
purpose of running for the California State Legislature,
raised funds and then decided not to run for office. You
discussed with Ms. Howard the legally permissible uses of the
funds held by your committee.

Specifically, you inquired about making a contribution to a
candidate for the California State Assembly and to a candidate for
the United States Congress. As Ms. Howard informed you, both of
these are legal uses of your campaign funds pursuant to the
personal use provisions of the Political Reform Act (Section 89510
et seq.). However, please be advised that you cannot transfer
funds from your campaign account to a candidate who is in a
jurisdiction which has valid contribution limits or to a candidate
who is running in a special election.

In addition, we recommend that you consult with the Federal
Elections Commission ("FEC") regarding the contribution from your
state campaign account to a federal candidate. The toll-free
advice number for the FEC is 1-800-424-9530.

I trust this answers your question.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Jill Stecher
Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:JS:ak

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question: Can a candidate who formed a committee for running for California State Legislature, raised funds, and then decided not to run, legally use those funds to make contributions to a candidate for the California State Assembly and to a candidate for the United States Congress?
sections.conclusion: Both contributions to a candidate for the California State Assembly and to a candidate for the United States Congress are legal uses of campaign funds pursuant to the personal use provisions of the Political Reform Act (Section 89510 et seq.). However, funds cannot be transferred to a candidate in a jurisdiction with valid contribution limits or to a candidate running in a special election. The requestor should consult with the Federal Elections Commission regarding the contribution to a federal candidate.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asks whether campaign funds raised for a failed legislative run can be legally contributed to candidates for the California State Assembly and U.S. Congress.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Both proposed contributions are permissible under California's Political Reform Act, but funds cannot be transferred to candidates in jurisdictions with contribution limits or special election candidates, and federal contribution rules should be verified with the FEC.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document confirms prior oral advice from Ms. Dixie Howard. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections with headers, but question and conclusion content are clearly present in letter body. Marked with 'SUPERSEDED' stamp. Minor OCR artifacts present but do not affect comprehension.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Can a candidate who formed a committee for running for California State Legislature, raised funds, and then decided not to run, legally use those funds to make contributions to a candidate for the California State Assembly and to a candidate for the United States Congress?

CONCLUSION: Both contributions to a candidate for the California State Assembly and to a candidate for the United States Congress are legal uses of campaign funds pursuant to the personal use provisions of the Political Reform Act (Section 89510 et seq.). However, funds cannot be transferred to a candidate in a jurisdiction with valid contribution limits or to a candidate running in a special election. The requestor should consult with the Federal Elections Commission regarding the contribution to a federal candidate.

You discussed with Ms. Howard the legally permissible uses of the funds held by your committee. embedding.summary: FPPC confirms oral advice that campaign funds from an unsuccessful legislative candidate can be contributed to state and federal candidates, subject to restrictions on contribution limit jurisdictions and special elections, with FEC consultation recommended for federal contributions.
